items.text: PSA for stressed folks: the Hollis Library basement is the quietest study spot on campus! Comfy chairs, zero noise, and barely anyone knows about it. The vending machine down there even sells $1 coffee. Open till midnight on weekdays. Grab a corner before finals week hits!

questions.stem: According to the post, what is available in the library basement?

options.A: A group tutoring session each evening
options.B: A printing station for student use
options.C: A reservation desk for study rooms

questions.explanation: The post mentions a vending machine that sells $1 coffee, which the correct option restates as inexpensive coffee from a machine.

questions.stem: The word "barely" in the post is closest in meaning to
questions.explanation: "Barely anyone knows about it" means almost no one knows, so "barely" is closest in meaning to hardly.

items.text: Heads up, everyone! The Media Lab in Kingston Hall is offering free printing all week until Friday, September 12. No page limit, black-and-white only. Just swipe your student ID at the front desk. Perfect timing for those end-of-term essays. Don't sleep on this deal!

questions.stem: According to the post, what is required to use the free printing?

options.D: Signing up on the lab website

questions.explanation: The post says to swipe your student ID at the front desk, which the correct option restates as scanning a valid student ID.

questions.stem: The word "swipe" in the post is closest in meaning to
questions.explanation: Here "swipe your student ID" refers to passing the card through a reader, so "swipe" is closest in meaning to scan.

items.text: Dear Ms. Alvarez, Your parking permit is ready for pickup at the Transportation Office, Room 118, before Friday, March 6. Bring your vehicle registration and a valid ID. Permits not collected by the deadline are released to the waitlist. Thank you, Campus Parking Services

questions.stem: According to the email, what must the recipient bring to collect the permit?

options.A: A recent parking fine payment receipt
options.C: A signed waitlist confirmation and deposit

questions.explanation: The email instructs the recipient to bring vehicle registration and a valid ID, which the correct option restates as registration papers and photo ID.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about a permit that is not picked up by March 6?

options.A: It will be mailed to the recipient's home
options.B: It will be given to someone else waiting
options.C: It can be collected the following week
options.D: It will be stored until the next semester

questions.explanation: The email states that permits not collected by the deadline are released to the waitlist, implying the permit goes to someone else waiting for one.

items.text: Hi Daniel, Thank you for signing up for our memory research study. Your session is booked for Tuesday, April 21, at 2:00 PM in Kessler Hall, Room 305. You'll receive $15 in cash afterward. Please arrive ten minutes early. Best, Dr. Okafor, Psychology Department

questions.stem: According to the email, what will Daniel receive for participating?

options.A: A gift card to the campus store
options.B: Course credit for a psychology class
options.D: A written summary of the results

questions.explanation: The email says Daniel will receive $15 in cash afterward, which the correct option restates as fifteen dollars paid in cash.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about a participant who arrives exactly at 2:00 PM?

options.A: The participant will receive extra compensation
options.B: The participant may reschedule the session
options.C: The participant will be later than requested
options.D: The participant must complete a consent form

questions.explanation: Because the email asks participants to arrive ten minutes early, someone arriving exactly at 2:00 PM would be later than the requested arrival time.
